public_api_changes.reason: non-blocking verification note; no external callers besides this package's own test file
public_api_changes.compile_impact: none outside engine/split (grep confirmed only orchestrate.go and orchestrate_test.go reference it)

internal_behavior_changes.before: on lease expiry: force-revert catalog Status Splitting->Active AND release FileGuard hold, then let BeginSplit retry TryAcquire
internal_behavior_changes.after: on lease expiry: force-revert catalog Status Splitting->Active only; never releases the guard; entire check-then-act sequence (including the transitionStatus WAL I/O) now runs under Orchestrator.mu, shared with EndSplit's own critical section, so the two can never interleave for the same fileID
internal_behavior_changes.risk: eliminates double-acquisition entirely; reintroduces 'future BeginSplit blocked forever' for a fileID whose true holder genuinely crashed (never calls EndSplit) -- explicitly documented tradeoff, not silently accepted

internal_behavior_changes.before: on TryAcquire loss, called reclaimIfExpired and retried TryAcquire if it returned true
internal_behavior_changes.after: on TryAcquire loss, calls reclaimIfExpired for its writer-unblocking side effect only, then always returns ErrAlreadySplitting (no retry, since reclaim no longer releases the guard)

internal_behavior_changes.before: transitionStatus call unlocked; guard.Release and clearLease deferred independently
internal_behavior_changes.after: transitionStatus call plus lease-entry delete now run together under Orchestrator.mu (the other half of the fencing pair with reclaimIfExpired); guard.Release remains a separate, unlocked defer (unaffected by outcome, same as before)

map[uint64]leaseEntry{deadline, gen, reclaimed} -- gen is a monotonically increasing per-Orchestrator counter (nextGen), reclaimed marks a lease that has already been force-reverted so repeated BeginSplit attempts for a still-abandoned fileID short-circuit instead of repeating a wasted transitionStatus call

test_impact.change: assertions updated: a second BeginSplit after lease expiry now correctly still returns ErrAlreadySplitting (guard stays held); catalog Status reverts to Active (writers unblocked); the true holder's eventual EndSplit returns ErrNotSplitting but still releases the guard; only then does a fresh BeginSplit succeed
new concurrency test; drives real goroutines racing BeginSplit against a fake-clock-expired-but-still-live H, asserting no iteration ever observes double-acquisition

new test targeting the disclosed test-coverage gap (disabling clearLease previously still passed); directly inspects the unexported leases map (white-box, same package) immediately after EndSplit to prove the entry is actually removed

downstream_consumers_checked: engine/split/split_race_test.go (compiles unchanged; full package test run confirms)
